requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks
- Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025.

3), a bank shall report its three largest daily values for gross

A bank providing correspondent banking services shall submit information in this

Intraday throughput - A bank that is direct participant shall report the daily
average in the reporting period of the percentage of its outgoing payments

requirements.full_sentence:
Scope of Intraday Liquidity Risk Monitoring
A bank shall manage its intraday liquidity risk on a system-by-system basis, i.e., for
each LVPS in a single currency.
requirements.object: risk on a system-by-system basis, i

Systems
(1) A bank which is a direct participant to an LVPS may manage its intraday
liquidity in different ways such as

(2) A direct participant shall apply a 'bottom-up' approach to determine the
appropriate basis for reporting the monitoring tools.
requirements.object: to determine the appropriate basis for reporting the monitoring tools

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall follow the
following principles:

(i) As a baseline, a bank shall report on each LVPS in which it participates
on a system-by-system-basis;
requirements.object: in which it participates on a system-by-system-basis

conditions:
Where a direct real-time technical liquidity bridge exists between two or
more LVPS

(ii) Where a direct real-time technical liquidity bridge exists between two or

At least one of the linked LVPS may therefore be considered
an ancillary system for the purpose of the tools;
requirements.object: ancillary system for the purpose of the tools

conditions: If a bank can demonstrate to the satisfaction of the DoS
requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) If a bank can demonstrate to the satisfaction of the DoS, RBI that it

transfer liquidity intraday between LVPSs which do not have a direct

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: the payment and settlement activity over its account(s) with its correspondent bank(s)

conditions: Where more than one correspondent bank is used

Where more than one correspondent bank is used,

conditions:
provided that the reporting bank can demonstrate to

For a bank which accesses an

(5) A bank which operates as direct participant of an LVPS but which also

requirements.object: purposes if the payments made directly through the LVPS and those made through the correspondent bank(s) are in the same

requirements.full_sentence:
Currency
(1) A bank that manages its intraday liquidity on a currency-by-currency basis

conditions: Where a bank can prove to the satisfaction of DoS
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where a bank can prove to the satisfaction of DoS, RBI, that it manages
liquidity on a cross-currency basis and has the ability to transfer funds

However, a bank shall also report at
an individual significant currency level in order to enable monitoring the

requirements.object: individual significant currency level in order to enable monitoring the extent to which it is reliant on foreign exchange swap marke

requirements.full_sentence:
Organisational structure
(1) A bank shall report intraday liquidity monitoring tools at consolidated as well

requirements.object: tools at consolidated as well as standalone ('solo') level

In cases of foreign bank operating in India as

requirements.object: branch level only

requirements.full_sentence:
Under the LCR framework,
a bank shall maintain an adequate level of unencumbered HQLA that can be
converted into cash to meet its liquidity needs for a 30 calendar days time horizon
under a significantly severe liquidity stress scenario specified by the Reserve
Bank.
requirements.object: of unencumbered HQLA that can be converted into cash to meet its liquidity needs for a 30 calendar days time horizon under

requirements.full_sentence:
At a minimum, the stock of liquid assets should be sufficient to enable a

requirements.full_sentence:
The LCR shall be used on an

requirements.object: ongoing basis to help monitor and control liquidity risk

A bank shall calculate LCR as:
Stock of high quality liquid assets (HQLA) Total net cash outflows over the next 30 calendar days
requirements.object: of high quality liquid assets (HQLA) Total net cash outflows over the next 30 calendar days

requirements.object: minimum of 100 per cent (i

As stock
of unencumbered HQLA is intended to serve as a defence against the potential
onset of liquidity stress, a bank shall strive to achieve a higher ratio than the
minimum prescribed above as an effort towards better liquidity risk management.
requirements.object: higher ratio than the minimum prescribed above as an effort towards better liquidity risk management

requirements.full_sentence:
However, during a period of financial stress, a bank shall have the option to use

requirements.object: use its stock of HQLA and thereby allowing LCR to fall below 100 per cent

requirements.full_sentence:
In such a

requirements.object: to DoS, RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The report shall
include reasons for such usage and corrective steps initiated to rectify the
situation.
requirements.object: usage and corrective steps initiated to rectify the situation

requirements.object: Indian bank on a standalone ('solo') as well as consolidated level

requirements.full_sentence:
For a foreign bank operating as branch in India,
the framework shall be applicable on standalone basis i.e., for Indian operations
only.
requirements.object: basis i

Such restrictions may arise from ring-fencing measures, non-
convertibility of local currency, and foreign exchange controls.
requirements.object: non- convertibility of local currency, and foreign exchange controls

requirements.full_sentence:
The banking
Group shall have processes in place to capture all liquidity transfer restrictions to

a whole.
requirements.object: to capture all liquidity transfer restrictions to the extent practicable, and to monitor the rules and regulations in the juri

requirements.object: the stock at the consolidated level to the extent that the related risks as measured by the legal entity's or sub-consolidated Gro

In assessing whether assets are freely transferable for regulatory purposes, a

requirements.object: assets may not be freely available to the consolidated entity due to regulatory, legal, tax, accounting, or other impedim

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: the liquidity needs in each significant currency

Accordingly, the
currencies of the stock of HQLA shall be similar in composition to the operational

requirements.object: to the operational needs of the bank

requirements.object: currencies may not remain transferable and convertible in a stress period, even for currencies that in normal times are f

When calculating the LCR on a consolidated basis, a cross-border banking

requirements.object: adopted in the home jurisdiction to all legal entities being consolidated

Home requirements for retail and SBC deposits shall apply to the relevant legal
entities (including branches of those entities) operating in host jurisdictions if:
(1) there are no host requirements for retail and SBC deposits in the particular
jurisdictions;
requirements.object: legal entities (including branches of those entities) operating in host jurisdictions if: (1) there are no host requirements for

or
(3) the home supervisor decides that home requirements should be used that
are stricter than the host requirements.
requirements.object: stricter than the host requirements

conditions:
if there is reasonable doubt about
the availability of liquidity
requirements.full_sentence:
The consolidated LCR shall reflect restrictions if there is reasonable doubt about

flows of a banking group, many of which are beyond the control of the Group.
requirements.object: is reasonable doubt about the availability of liquidity, due to factors that can impede cross-border liquidity flows of

requirements.full_sentence:
Some of these restrictions may not be clearly incorporated into law or may
become visible only in times of stress.
requirements.object: law or may become visible only in times of stress

conditions:
If the HQLA held in excess of the total net cash outflows are
not transferable
requirements.full_sentence:
If the HQLA held in excess of the total net cash outflows are
not transferable, such surplus liquidity shall be excluded from consolidated LCR.
requirements.object: LCR

requirements.full_sentence:
A banking Group shall have processes in place to capture all liquidity transfer
restrictions to the extent practicable, and to monitor the rules and regulations in
the jurisdictions in which the Group operates and assess their liquidity
implications for the Group as a whole.
requirements.object: to capture all liquidity transfer restrictions to the extent practicable, and to monitor the rules and regulations in the juri

requirements.full_sentence:
Assets qualifying as HQLA should be liquid in markets during a time of stress
and ideally be central bank eligible (except Level 2B assets as defined in
subsequent paragraphs) for intraday and overnight liquidity facilities.
requirements.object: during a time of stress and ideally be central bank eligible (except Level 2B assets as defined in subsequent paragraphs) for int

of generating liquidity without incurring large discounts due to fire-sale discounts
even in times of stress.
requirements.object: liquidity without incurring large discounts due to fire-sale discounts even in times of stress

requirements.full_sentence:
All assets in the stock of HQLA shall possess certain fundamental characteristics
and market related characteristics, and are subject to certain operational
requirements, as prescribed below.
requirements.object: and market related characteristics, and are subject to certain operational requirements, as prescribed bel

requirements.full_sentence:
Characteristics
contributing to reduced risk and enhanced liquidity may include the high credit
standing of the issuer, low degree of subordination, low duration, low legal risk,
low inflation risk, and denomination in a convertible currency with low foreign
exchange risk.
requirements.object: standing of the issuer, low degree of subordination, low duration, low legal risk, low inflation risk, and denomination in a c

requirements.full_sentence:
Ease and certainty of valuation: In order to qualify as HQLA, an asset should
have standardised, homogenous, and simple structure, making it more fungible.
requirements.object: simple structure, making it more fungible

requirements.full_sentence:
The pricing formula of such an asset must be easy to calculate, transparent, in
use by industry practitioners, and not depend on strong assumptions.
requirements.object: transparent, in use by industry practitioners, and not depend on strong assumptions

requirements.full_sentence:
The inputs
into the pricing formula must also be publicly available.

Based on these criteria,
a bank shall not include structured or exotic products in its stock of HQLA.
requirements.object: products in its stock of HQLA

requirements.full_sentence:
Low correlation with risky assets: Assets in the stock of HQLA should have low
correlation with risky assets.
requirements.object: risky assets

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not include assets with wrong-way
(highly correlated) risk in the stock of HQLA.
requirements.object: (highly correlated) risk in the stock of HQLA

requirements.full_sentence:
Active and sizable market: Assets included in the stock of HQLA should have
active outright sale or repo market at all times, evidenced by historical market
breadth and market depth e.g., low bid-ask spreads, high trading volumes, and
<image>
a large and diverse number of market participants.
requirements.object: or repo market at all times, evidenced by historical market breadth and market depth e

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, there should be
robust market infrastructure, including multiple committed market makers for the
asset.
requirements.object: including multiple committed market makers for the asset

requirements.full_sentence:
Low volatility: Assets included in the stock of HQLA should exhibit low volatility,
as reflected by traded prices and spreads.
requirements.object: reflected by traded prices and spreads

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be historical evidence of
relative stability of market terms such as consistent prices and haircuts, and
trading volumes during stressed periods.
requirements.object: relative stability of market terms such as consistent prices and haircuts, and trading volumes during stressed periods

requirements.full_sentence:

should ideally be eligible at central bank of the relevant jurisdictions for intraday

requirements.object: central bank of the relevant jurisdictions for intraday liquidity needs and overnight liquidity facilities

requirements.full_sentence:

central bank eligibility does not by itself constitute the basis for the categorisation

requirements.object: bank eligibility does not by itself constitute the basis for the categorisation of an asset as HQLA

requirements.full_sentence:

mingled / used as hedges on trading positions;
requirements.object: used as hedges on trading positions

requirements.full_sentence:
The assets shall be managed with sole intent for use as a
source of contingent funds.
requirements.object: intent for use as a source of contingent funds

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall periodically monetise a representative proportion of the assets in its
stock of HQLA through repo or outright sale, in order to test its access to the
market, the effectiveness of processes for monetisation, the availability of the
assets, and to minimise the risk of negative signalling during a period of actual
stress.
requirements.object: proportion of the assets in its stock of HQLA through repo or outright sale, in order to test its access to the

requirements.full_sentence: All assets in the stock shall be unencumbered.

An asset in the stock shall neither
be pledged (either explicitly or implicitly) to secure, collateralise, or credit-
enhance any transaction, nor be designated to cover operational costs (such as
rents and salaries).
requirements.object: explicitly or implicitly) to secure, collateralise, or credit- enhance any transaction, nor be designated to cover operatio

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition, assets which qualify for the stock of HQLA that have been
pre-positioned or deposited with, or pledged to, the central bank or a Public
Sector Entity (PSE) but have not been used to generate liquidity shall also be
included in the stock.
requirements.object: the stock

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a bank shall not include in the stock of HQLA any
assets, or liquidity generated from assets, which it has received under right of re-
hypothecation or re-repo, if the beneficial owner has the contractual right to
withdraw those assets during the 30-day stress period.
requirements.object: of HQLA any assets, or liquidity generated from assets, which it has received under right of re- hypothecation or re-repo

conditions:
provided that bank records an appropriate outflow for the

Eligible assets received as collateral for derivatives transactions that are not
segregated and are legally able to be rehypothecated may be included in the

associated risks, as set out in these Directions.
requirements.object: outflow for the associated risks, as set out in these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The stock of HQLA shall be under the control of the function charged with
managing the liquidity of the bank (e.g., the Treasurer).
requirements.object: of the function charged with managing the liquidity of the bank (e

requirements.full_sentence:
This function shall have
the continuous authority, and legal and operational capability to monetise any
asset in the stock.
requirements.object: and legal and operational capability to monetise any asset in the stock

requirements.full_sentence:
Such control shall be evidenced either by maintaining assets
in a separate pool managed by the function with the sole intent for use as a
source of contingent funds, or by demonstrating that the function can monetise
the asset at any point in the 30-day stress period and that the proceeds of doing
<image>
so are available to the function throughout the 30-day stress period without
directly conflicting with a stated business or risk management strategy.
requirements.object: maintaining assets in a separate pool managed by the function with the sole intent for use as a source of contingent funds, or

conditions: if the sale of that asset
requirements.full_sentence:
An asset
shall not be included in the stock if the sale of that asset, without replacement
throughout the 30-day period, would remove a hedge that would create an open
risk position in excess of internal limits.

conditions:
If a bank does not have the necessary operational capability to monetise an
HQLA to meet outflows during the stress period
requirements.full_sentence:
If a bank does not have the necessary operational capability to monetise an
HQLA to meet outflows during the stress period, it shall not include the same in
the stock of HQLA, even if the HQLA meets the definition of 'unencumbered'
provided in paragraph 4.
requirements.object: the stock of HQLA, even if the HQLA meets the definition of 'unencumbered' provided in paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence:
Monetisation of the
asset must be executable, from an operational perspective, in the standard
settlement period for the asset class in the relevant jurisdiction.
requirements.object: operational perspective, in the standard settlement period for the asset class in the relevant jurisdiction

conditions:
If a bank chooses to hedge
the market risk of an HQLA
requirements.full_sentence:
If a bank chooses to hedge
the market risk of an HQLA, the market value of the HQLA shall be suitably
adjusted to reflect the cash outflow that would arise if the hedge were to be
closed out early (in the event of the asset being sold).
requirements.object: reflect the cash outflow that would arise if the hedge were to be closed out early (in the event of the asset being sold)

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have a policy in place that identifies legal entities, geographical
locations, currencies, and specific custodial or bank accounts where HQLA are
held.
requirements.object: place that identifies legal entities, geographical locations, currencies, and specific custodial or bank accounts where HQLA are held

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition, the bank shall determine whether for operational reasons any
such assets should be excluded and therefore, have the ability to determine the
composition of its stock on a daily basis.
requirements.object: reasons any such assets should be excluded and therefore, have the ability to determine the composition of its stock

conditions:
In case certain eligible asset classes can be monetised through outright sale only
due to absence of large

where there are impediments to sale such
as fire-sale discounts which may lead to breach of minimum solvency
requirements
requirements.full_sentence:
In case certain eligible asset classes can be monetised through outright sale only
due to absence of large, deep, and active repo markets, a bank shall exclude
such assets from the stock of HQLA, where there are impediments to sale such
as fire-sale discounts which may lead to breach of minimum solvency
requirements, or such assets for which there is a requirement to hold (e.g.,
statutory minimum inventory requirements for market making).

encumbrance.
requirements.object: the term of encumbrance

requirements.full_sentence:
The term of encumbrance should be determined taking into
account the bank's ability to freely dispose off or exchange such assets and
the term of the liability of the bank's customer that generates the
segregation requirement.
requirements.object: account the bank's ability to freely dispose off or exchange such assets and the term of the liability of the bank's custome

requirements.full_sentence:
For secured funding arrangements, a bank shall use its balance sheet and
accounting principles such that:
(1) Securities borrowed in securities financing transactions (such as reverse
repos and collateral swaps) shall be excluded from a bank's asset if it does
not have beneficial ownership.
requirements.object: and accounting principles such that: (1) Securities borrowed in securities financing transactions (such as reverse repos and col

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Securities lent in securities financing transactions shall be included in the
bank's assets if a bank retains beneficial ownership.
requirements.object: bank's assets if a bank retains beneficial ownership

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Securities received through collateral swaps shall not be included in a
bank's assets if those securities do not appear on its Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: bank's assets if those securities do not appear on its Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) Where a bank has encumbered securities in repos or other securities
financing transactions but has retained beneficial ownership and the
securities remain on its balance sheet, such securities shall be allocated to
the appropriate RSF category according to its characteristics such as HQLA
classification, maturity and issuer.
requirements.object: appropriate RSF category according to its characteristics such as HQLA classification, maturity and issuer

requirements.full_sentence:
The treatment for the amount receivable by a bank under reverse repo
transaction shall be same as applicable to any other loan.
requirements.object: to any other loan

requirements.full_sentence:
The applicable RSF
factor shall depend on the counterparty and term of the operation, subject to
following exceptions:
(1) Reverse repos with financial institutions that have residual maturity of less
than six months and secured by level 1 assets shall receive a 10 per cent
RSF factor as per paragraph 232.
requirements.object: and term of the operation

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Reverse repos with financial institutions that have residual maturity of less
than six months and secured by other assets shall receive a 15 per cent
RSF factor as per paragraph 233.
requirements.object: cent RSF factor as per paragraph 233

requirements.full_sentence:
Amounts receivables and payable under securities financing transactions shall
be reported on a gross basis with the gross amount of such receivables and
payables reported on the RSF side and ASF side, respectively.
requirements.object: gross basis with the gross amount of such receivables and payables reported on the RSF side and ASF side, respectively

requirements.full_sentence:
The only
exception is securities financing transactions with a single counterparty which
may be reported on a net basis for NSFR calculation, provided the netting
conditions set out in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025 are met.
requirements.object: net basis for NSFR calculation

requirements.full_sentence:
For loans which are only partially secured and are therefore separated into
secured and unsecured portions with different risk weights under Basel
Framework, the specific characteristics of these portions of loans shall be taken
into account for the calculation of the NSFR.
requirements.object: for the calculation of the NSFR

requirements.full_sentence:
The secured and unsecured
portions of a loan shall be treated according to its characteristics and assigned
the corresponding RSF factor.
requirements.object: its characteristics and assigned the corresponding RSF factor

conditions:
If it is not possible to draw the distinction between
the secured and unsecured part of the loan
requirements.full_sentence:
If it is not possible to draw the distinction between
the secured and unsecured part of the loan, the higher RSF factor shall apply to
the whole loan.
requirements.object: loan

requirements.full_sentence:
Where an
eligible bilateral netting contract is in place that meets the conditions as specified
in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025, the net replacement cost may be used for the set of
derivative exposures covered by the contract.
requirements.object: set of derivative exposures covered by the contract

requirements.full_sentence:
In calculating NSFR derivative assets (i.e., derivative assets - cash collateral
received as variation margin on derivative assets), collateral received in
connection with derivative contracts may not offset the positive replacement cost
amount, regardless of whether or not netting is permitted under the bank's
operative accounting or risk-based framework, unless it is received in the form of
cash variation margin and meets the conditions as specified in Reserve Bank of
India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions,
requirements.object: cost amount, regardless of whether or not netting is permitted under the bank's operative accounting or risk-based framework

requirements.full_sentence:
Any remaining balance sheet liability associated with (a) variation margin
received that does not meet the criteria above or (b) initial margin received may
not offset derivative assets and shall be assigned a zero per cent ASF factor.
requirements.object: per cent ASF factor

requirements.full_sentence:
If an on-balance sheet asset is associated with collateral posted as initial margin
and is reflected as such to the extent under the bank's accounting framework,
the asset shall not be counted as an encumbered asset in the calculation of a
bank's RSF under NSFR to avoid double-counting.
requirements.object: encumbered asset in the calculation of a bank's RSF under NSFR to avoid double-counting

requirements.full_sentence:
For NSFR disclosure template and related instructions a bank shall refer to
relevant instructions in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Financial
Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall carry out both the
analyses.
requirements.object: analyses

requirements.full_sentence:
The framework, both DGA and TGA, shall be applied to the global position
(domestic and overseas branches) of assets, liabilities, and OBS items of a bank,
which are rate sensitive.
requirements.object: global position (domestic and overseas branches) of assets, liabilities, and OBS items of a bank, which are rate sensitive

conditions: where either the assets
requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute its IRR position in each currency
applying the DGA and TGA to the Rate Sensitive Assets (RSA)/ Rate Sensitive
Liabilities (RSL) items, where either the assets, or liabilities in a currency
constitute 5 per cent or more of bank's global assets or global liabilities.
requirements.object: in each currency applying the DGA and TGA to the Rate Sensitive Assets (RSA)/ Rate Sensitive Liabilities (RSL) items

requirements.full_sentence:
The IRR
position in all other residual currencies shall be computed separately on an
aggregate basis.
requirements.object: an aggregate basis

requirements.full_sentence: The methodologies specified in these Directions shall serve as a benchmark.

A
bank which has more sophisticated systems may continue its existing systems
but shall also adopt the DGA and TGA as supervisory reporting / disclosure
frameworks.
requirements.object: and TGA as supervisory reporting / disclosure frameworks

requirements.full_sentence:
The Gap Report shall be generated by grouping rate sensitive liabilities, assets
and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity
or next repricing period, whichever is earlier.
requirements.object: rate sensitive liabilities, assets and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity or next r

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall use the Traditional Gap Analysis to measure the level of a bank's
exposure to IRR in terms of sensitivity of its NII to interest rate movements over
the horizon of analysis, which is usually one year.
requirements.object: Analysis to measure the level of a bank's exposure to IRR in terms of sensitivity of its NII to interest rate movements over t

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall bucket all RSA,
RSL and OBS items as per residual maturity / re-pricing date in various time
buckets as per guidance provided in Annex VII and report as per the reporting
format of Interest Rate Sensitivity statement provided in Annex-III.
requirements.object: and OBS items as per residual maturity / re-pricing date in various time buckets as per guidance provided in Annex VII and report

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall
compute Earnings at Risk (EaR) representing the loss of income under different
interest rate scenarios over a time horizon of one year.
requirements.object: (EaR) representing the loss of income under different interest rate scenarios over a time horizon of one year

requirements.full_sentence:
For calculation of changes in MVE due to changes in the interest rates, market
values of RSA and RSL shall be used.

However, for simplicity, the book values
of the RSA and RSL (both inclusive of notional value of rate sensitive OBS items)
may be used as an approximation.
requirements.object: approximation

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall bucket all RSA and RSL, including the rate sensitive OBS asset and
liabilities, as per residual maturity / re-pricing dates in various time buckets as
per the guidance provided in Annex VII, and compute the Modified Duration Gap
(MDG).
requirements.object: RSL, including the rate sensitive OBS asset and liabilities, as per residual maturity / re-pricing dates in various time buckets as

requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG shall be used to evaluate the impact on the MVE of the bank
under different interest rate scenarios.
requirements.object: the impact on the MVE of the bank under different interest rate scenarios

requirements.full_sentence:
Under MDG framework a bank shall compute Modified Duration of RSA (MDA)
and Modified Duration of RSL (MDL).
requirements.object: RSA (MDA) and Modified Duration of RSL (MDL)

requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG, reflecting the degree of duration mismatch in the RSA and RSL in a
bank's balance sheet, shall be calculated with the help of the following formula:

A higher absolute value of the MDG shall indicate greater exposure of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The estimated drop in MVE resulting from the prescribed interest rate shock shall
indicate the economic impact on a bank's equity, assuming the shock scenario
<image>
materialises.
requirements.object: on a bank's equity, assuming the shock scenario <image> materialises

requirements.full_sentence:
This decline shall not be construed as an accounting loss as
banking book is not marked to market.
requirements.object: accounting loss as banking book is not marked to market

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a bank that
has the capability to use market value of assets and liabilities of banking book
shall do so.

The market values of assets and liabilities shall be determined by
discounted cash flow method.
requirements.object: cash flow method

requirements.full_sentence:
The
absolute notional amount of rate sensitive off-balance sheet items in each
time bucket shall be included as RSA if positive and as RSL if negative.
requirements.object: if positive and as RSL if negative

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The mid-point of each time bucket shall be taken as a proxy for the maturity
of assets and liabilities in that time bucket, except for those for which the
bank is able to compute modified duration on individual basis.
requirements.object: proxy for the maturity of assets and liabilities in that time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
The requirement to determine the impact of IRR on the Market Value Equity
(MVE) shall be applicable across the bank's entire balance sheet, i.e., both the
banking and trading books.
requirements.object: bank's entire balance sheet, i

requirements.full_sentence:
This requirement shall be in addition to the existing
guidelines for assessing capital adequacy requirement for interest rate sensitive
positions in the trading book and banking book (under Pillar II) separately.
requirements.object: the existing guidelines for assessing capital adequacy requirement for interest rate sensitive positions in the trading book and ban

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be considered an outlier from a supervisory perspective (under Pillar
II) if the economic value of its banking book declines by more than 20 per cent of
its MVE due to a standardised interest rate shock of 200 basis points.
requirements.object: from a supervisory perspective (under Pillar II) if the economic value of its banking book declines by more than 20 per cent

requirements.full_sentence:
However,
for decline in the MVE due to standardised interest rate shock of 200 basis points
on the entire balance sheet, a bank shall not be subjected to outlier criteria.
requirements.object: criteria

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The 200 basis point shock shall be applied to the entire balance
sheet, including the trading book.
requirements.object: entire balance sheet, including the trading book

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall monitor its IRR positions and take
appropriate corrective action with reference to the stipulations in paragraph 274
for internal limits on volatility of MVE, i.e., percentage variation in the MVE, and
limits on individual gaps.
requirements.object: and take appropriate corrective action with reference to the stipulations in paragraph 274 for internal limits on volatility

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare the Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, under TGA
and DGA, as per reporting format provided in Annex-III, bucketing all RSA and
RSL in following time buckets as per table below:
Sr.
requirements.object: Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, under TGA and DGA, as per reporting format provided in Annex-III, bucketing all RSA and RSL in

requirements.full_sentence:
The RSA and RSL with fixed maturities shall be classified in the relevant time
buckets based on residual maturity / re-pricing dates.
requirements.object: relevant time buckets based on residual maturity / re-pricing dates

requirements.full_sentence:
For RSA and RSL items without fixed maturity or with embedded optionality (i.e.,
savings bank deposits, current account deposits and mortgage loans), a bank
shall undertake behavioural studies to assess the interest rate sensitivity
realistically.
requirements.object: assess the interest rate sensitivity realistically

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have an appropriate process and a detailed
framework for conducting such studies and review their output at least annually.
requirements.object: and a detailed framework for conducting such studies and review their output at least annually

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall apply the results of the behavioural studies on a consistent basis
and the results shall be reviewed / revised once a year in the first quarter of the
financial year, if necessary.
requirements.object: the behavioural studies on a consistent basis and the results shall be reviewed / revised once a year in the first quarter of the financial year

requirements.full_sentence:
The behavioural studies shall be based on at least
three years data.
requirements.object: least three years data

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall evolve a suitable mechanism, supported by
empirical studies and behavioural analysis to estimate the future behaviour of
assets, liabilities and OBS items with respect to changes in market variables.
requirements.object: supported by empirical studies and behavioural analysis to estimate the future behaviour of assets, liabilities and OBS i

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, a bank having the capability
to compute the MD of each item of RSA and RSL shall do so.

However, a bank which is not equipped to compute the MD of each individual
rate sensitive asset, liability and off-balance sheet position shall:
(1) group RSA and RSL under the broad categories indicated in Annex VII
under various time buckets;
requirements.object: and RSL under the broad categories indicated in Annex VII under various time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG computed from this approach shall be simpler, providing a cost
effective alternative despite approximations in the calculation of MD.
requirements.object: cost effective alternative despite approximations in the calculation of MD

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compile the ALM statements and compute the MDG for the entire
balance sheet, encompassing both the banking and trading books.
requirements.object: and compute the MDG for the entire balance sheet, encompassing both the banking and trading books

requirements.full_sentence:
While reporting the currency-wise IRR position, as advised in paragraph 252
above, in Interest Rate Sensitivity statement provided in Annex-III, a bank shall
convert the foreign currencies into ₹ using the relevant spot closing rates as
published by FEDAI.
requirements.object: into ₹ using the relevant spot closing rates as published by FEDAI

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute the MD of each item or group of items
of rate sensitive assets, liabilities and OBS items using the appropriate coupon
and relevant foreign currency yield curve.
requirements.object: each item or group of items of rate sensitive assets, liabilities and OBS items using the appropriate coupon and relevant foreign cu

requirements.full_sentence:
For residual currencies, a bank may
use the appropriate coupon and yield curve of the largest among the residual
currencies.
requirements.object: and yield curve of the largest among the residual currencies

requirements.full_sentence:
In deciding the appropriate coupon and yield curves, a bank shall
follow the principles outlined in Annex VII.
requirements.object: in Annex VII

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall, with the approval of its Board / RMC, set appropriate internal limits
on Earnings at Risk (EaR) and volatility in the MVE, based on its risk bearing and
risk management capacity.
requirements.object: of its Board / RMC, set appropriate internal limits on Earnings at Risk (EaR) and volatility in the MVE, based on its risk bearing

requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be linked to MVE for DGA and the
NII for TGA.
requirements.object: for DGA and the NII for TGA

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board / ALCO shall periodically review the above limits after
assessing various scenarios of interest rates and the resultant volatility of
earnings in terms of NII and volatility in Net Worth.
requirements.object: limits after assessing various scenarios of interest rates and the resultant volatility of earnings in terms of NII and

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO prior approval shall be obtained for deciding upon yields, assumptions
used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, etc.
requirements.object: upon yields, assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank
shall ensure that such decisions are compliant with regulatory guidelines.
requirements.object: are compliant with regulatory guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall update material assumptions on a regular basis to reflect the current
market and operating environment.
requirements.object: a regular basis to reflect the current market and operating environment

requirements.full_sentence:
The process for developing such material
assumptions shall be formalized and reviewed at least annually.
requirements.object: at least annually

conditions:
if an interest rate shock of 200
basis points results in a drop in the value of equity of more than 20 per cent of
MVE
requirements.full_sentence:
In accordance with Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, if an interest rate shock of 200
basis points results in a drop in the value of equity of more than 20 per cent of
MVE, the level of IRR shall be treated as excessive.

In such cases, the RBI may
require the bank to take corrective action as specified in the above Directions.
requirements.object: take corrective action as specified in the above Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall measure its vulnerability to losses in stressed market conditions,
including the breakdown of key assumptions, and consider these results when
establishing and reviewing its limits and policies in respect of IRR, in accordance
<image>
with the instructions provided in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: losses in stressed market conditions, including the breakdown of key assumptions, and consider these results when establi

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall adopt the practice of periodic model validation.
requirements.object: periodic model validation

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where
internal models / software packages are used, the bank shall ensure the integrity
and validation of data / assumptions being used to generate the results,
validation and functioning of the entire system of IRR management by an
independent audit either by an experienced internal auditor or external auditor
who is conversant with risk management processes.
requirements.object: validation of data / assumptions being used to generate the results, validation and functioning of the entire system of IRR m

requirements.full_sentence:
The Audit Committee of the
Board (ACB) shall ensure the suitability of auditors through a due diligence
process.
requirements.object: auditors through a due diligence process

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure documentation in respect of discount rates, coupons,
assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies and
validation process.
requirements.object: of discount rates, coupons, assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies and validation proc

requirements.full_sentence:
All material assumptions, regardless of the source, shall be
supported with analysis and documentation.
requirements.object: and documentation

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure that sufficient
documentation is made available at the time of validation, internal audit, statutory
audit and RBI inspection.
requirements.object: is made available at the time of validation, internal audit, statutory audit and RBI inspection

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare domestic structural liquidity statement (SLS) - ₹ on a daily
basis.
requirements.object: statement (SLS) - ₹ on a daily basis

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall report the liquidity return comprising the SLS in five parts, viz.,
(1) Domestic currency for Indian operations
(2) Foreign currency for Indian operations
(3) Combined Indian operations
(4) Overseas branch operations
(5) Consolidated bank operations
The formats of the returns are provided in Annex-I.
requirements.object: comprising the SLS in five parts, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to the periodicity of submission in respect of each part of the
Liquidity Return is specified in the table below.
requirements.object: of submission in respect of each part of the Liquidity Return is specified in the table below

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve
Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, dated February
27, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory returns.
requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, dated February 27, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory ret

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit the Basel III Liquidity Returns (BLR) at the frequencies
specified in the table below.
requirements.object: Liquidity Returns (BLR) at the frequencies specified in the table below

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall report LCR and NSFR positions as on
date of reporting in monthly BLR-1 and quarterly BLR-6, respectively.
requirements.object: positions as on date of reporting in monthly BLR-1 and quarterly BLR-6, respectively

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the stipulated periodicity of submission of statement on LCR to
<image>
Reserve Bank, a bank shall develop operational capacity to increase the
periodicity to weekly or even daily in stressed situations, if so directed by the RBI.
requirements.object: increase the periodicity to weekly or even daily in stressed situations

requirements.full_sentence:
The time lag in reporting shall be as short as feasible, notwithstanding the outer
limits prescribed.
requirements.object: feasible, notwithstanding the outer limits prescribed

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve Bank of India (Filing of
Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory
returns
Sl.
requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory returns Sl

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare the IRS Statement, under TGA and DGA, as per reporting
format of provided in Annex-III.
requirements.object: under TGA and DGA, as per reporting format of provided in Annex-III

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit IRS Statement under TGA and DGA approaches on a
monthly basis.
requirements.object: TGA and DGA approaches on a monthly basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve Bank of India (Filing of
Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory
returns.
requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory returns

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and
guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

A bank should also report figures in respect of subsidiaries / joint ventures in the same format on a
stand-alone basis, i.e., these figures should not be reckoned while preparing country-wise reports.

A bank may use exchange rates published by Reuters/
Bloomberg etc.
